fix(jetson): rewrite step 8 with temp file + sys.argv (no env vars)

Previous approaches all failed because:
- Shell grep+cut: CRLF issues from Windows .env files
- Heredoc + env vars: export order wrong, then still failed
- os.environ: unreliable across sudo boundaries

New approach is bulletproof:
- Write Python script to /tmp/_agrikd_inject_creds.py
- Pass cfg_path and repo_root as sys.argv[1] and sys.argv[2]
- No env vars, no heredoc, no shell variable capture
- Also use cat+heredoc for minimal config (not python3 -c)
- Verbose debug output at every step
- Cleanup temp file after execution

fix(jetson): export env vars BEFORE heredoc + rewrite step 8

Root cause: CFG_PATH and REPO_ROOT_PATH were exported AFTER
the Python heredoc, so os.environ read empty strings.

Rewrite step 8 as a single robust Python script:
- Export env vars before heredoc (not after!)
- Python reads .env files directly (no grep/cut/shell issues)
- Handles CRLF, encoding, missing files gracefully
- Prints debug: config path, repo root, which files found/missing
- Verifies write succeeded with re-read
- Clean fallback: exit(0)=ok, exit(2)=interactive, else=error

Replace custom MemoryHookProvider implementation with built-in
AgentCoreMemorySessionManager. Key changes:

* fix(notebook): Configure memory mode and inject memory_id to prevent runtime failures

configure() defaults to memory_mode="NO_MEMORY", so the auto-created
execution role has no memory IAM permissions — causing ListMemoryEvents
failures at runtime. Additionally, the toolkit doesn't know about the
manually-created memory resource, so it provisions a duplicate on launch.

Fix: Set memory_mode="STM_ONLY" in configure() and inject the existing
memory_id into .bedrock_agentcore.yaml before launch(). Both issues only
exist because the tutorial manually creates resources that the toolkit
normally manages end-to-end.

Fix Ubuntu CI build: use _WIN32 guard for io.h, fix mode_t va_arg promotion

test_stdio.c used __APPLE_CC__ to detect macOS, leaving the Windows-only
<io.h> included on Linux too, causing a fatal compile error in CI.

Replace the guard with _WIN32 so only Windows gets the MSVC headers;
macOS and Linux both use the project's own "unistd.h" which already
defines all needed O_* flags and S_* mode constants.

Also fix unistd.c: va_arg(argp, mode_t) triggers a GCC warning because
mode_t (unsigned short) is promoted to int through variadic calls.
Change to (mode_t)va_arg(argp, int) to match the C standard.

commit.message:
fix: garbled output on POSIX/C locale systems

On fresh VPS installs the system locale is often C or POSIX (ASCII
only). UTF-8 bytes for symbols like ✔ ⚠ ✘ ── and Chinese text are
output as raw multi-byte sequences, appearing as garbled characters
(乱码) in the terminal.

Fix: add _setup_locale() called before set -euo pipefail that picks
the best available UTF-8 locale in order: C.UTF-8 → en_US.UTF-8 →
bare LANG=en_US.UTF-8 fallback.  C.UTF-8 is available on all modern
glibc systems (Debian/Ubuntu/RHEL 8+) and is preferred because it
avoids pulling in full locale data.

Also add DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive to apt-get calls to suppress
interactive locale-related prompts during dependency installation.

Bump version to 0.2.7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

gh 2.89.0 promotes 'gh copilot' to a built-in command. The previous

* fix(copilot-cli): use 'gh copilot -- --help' for idempotency check

On gh 2.89.0+, 'gh copilot --help' exits 0 even before the Copilot
CLI binary has been downloaded — it shows gh's own wrapper help and
never touches the binary. This caused the idempotency check to always
short-circuit to 'already available' on a fresh system.

Fix: use 'gh copilot -- --help' instead. The '--' passes the flag
through to the actual Copilot CLI binary, so:
  - binary already present → exits 0 immediately (idempotent)
  - binary not yet downloaded → triggers proactive download, then exits 0
  - older gh without extension → exits non-zero → falls through to install

Also update the file-header comment to document this nuance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(devcontainer): strip CRLF from .sh files on container create (#69) (#71)

Add onCreateCommand to strip carriage returns from all shell scripts
before postCreateCommand runs. This guards against the case where a
Windows user's working tree still has CRLF line endings after the
PR #66 gitattributes fix, which updates git's index but NOT the
working tree.

  find . -name '*.sh' | xargs sed -i 's/\r//'

- Safe on Linux: no-op if files are already LF
- Defensive against future CRLF drift from any Windows checkout
- No-op in GitHub Codespaces (fresh clone is always LF)
- Runs BEFORE postCreateCommand so bash setup.sh never sees \r

ships Python 3.8. Flask 3.1.3 requires >=3.9 causing Docker build failure.

Downgraded to Python 3.8-compatible versions:
- flask 3.1.3 → 3.0.3 (last version supporting Python 3.8)
- waitress 3.0.1 → 2.1.3 (last version supporting Python 3.8)
- requests 2.33.0 → 2.31.0 (compatible with Python 3.8)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Reality private key empty — use full path for xray x25519

gen_reality_keys() was calling 'xray x25519' without the full path
/usr/local/bin/xray.  On Debian/Ubuntu root shells /usr/local/bin is
often absent from PATH, so the command failed silently (stderr was
redirected to /dev/null), leaving private_key/public_key as empty
strings in state.json.  Xray then rejected the config with
'empty privateKey' (exit code 23).

Fixes:
- gen_reality_keys: use /usr/local/bin/xray x25519 (full path)
- gen_reality_keys: switch awk '{print $3}' to '{print $NF}' to
  handle any whitespace variation in xray output
- gen_reality_keys: validate extracted keys are non-empty and print
  the raw output on failure for easier debugging
- setup_vless_reality: add '|| return 1' after gen_reality_keys so
  the setup aborts immediately instead of writing a broken config
- generate_config_vless_reality: validate private_key non-empty
  before generating config, giving a clear actionable error message

Bump version to 0.2.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix logout: capture id_token before cookie sign-out

The /logout endpoint was signing out of the cookie scheme first, which
destroyed the authentication ticket containing the saved tokens. When
the OIDC sign-out then ran, it could not find the id_token to send as
id_token_hint to Keycloak, causing a 'Missing parameters: id_token_hint'
error page.

Fix: capture the id_token via GetTokenAsync before any sign-out, then
pass it to the OIDC sign-out via both AuthenticationProperties.Items
and StoreTokens so the OIDC handler can include it in the logout request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(copilot-cli): use 'gh copilot -- --help' for idempotency check

On gh 2.89.0+, 'gh copilot --help' exits 0 even before the Copilot
CLI binary has been downloaded — it shows gh's own wrapper help and
never touches the binary. This caused the idempotency check to always
short-circuit to 'already available' on a fresh system.

Fix: use 'gh copilot -- --help' instead. The '--' passes the flag
through to the actual Copilot CLI binary, so:
  - binary already present → exits 0 immediately (idempotent)
  - binary not yet downloaded → triggers proactive download, then exits 0
  - older gh without extension → exits non-zero → falls through to install

Also update the file-header comment to document this nuance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(shell): merge stderr into stdout for ordered output in piped environments (#68) (#70)

Add \xec 2>&1\ immediately after \set -euo pipefail\ in both
setup.sh and scripts/linux/setup.sh. This merges stderr into stdout
so log_error() output appears in-sequence with all other log output
when running inside a Devcontainer or piped environment.

The two tool scripts in scripts/linux/tools/ have no >&2 calls
and inherit the merged file descriptor from the parent — no changes
needed there.

Fixes #68

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(devcontainer): strip CRLF from .sh files on container create (#69) (#71)

Add onCreateCommand to strip carriage returns from all shell scripts
before postCreateCommand runs. This guards against the case where a
Windows user's working tree still has CRLF line endings after the
PR #66 gitattributes fix, which updates git's index but NOT the
working tree.

  find . -name '*.sh' | xargs sed -i 's/\r//'

- Safe on Linux: no-op if files are already LF
- Defensive against future CRLF drift from any Windows checkout
- No-op in GitHub Codespaces (fresh clone is always LF)
- Runs BEFORE postCreateCommand so bash setup.sh never sees \r

Fixes #69
